spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Chrome removes support for obsolete virtual cameras on macOS surface (macOS, DAL); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: third-party.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "Scan with getUserMedia consent"; "Clear" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5086798634156032.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on device-inventory-diff
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: device-inventory-diff covers the happy path of Chrome removes support for obsolete virtual cameras on macOS and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
